# Generated by google (gemini-2.5-flash)
# Prompt: Create a GitHub Actions workflow that accepts custom build commands as workflow inputs. Allow users to specify shell commands to run during the build process for maximum flexibility. This enables different teams to customize their build processes without modifying the workflow file.
# Category: cicd_security

on:
  workflow_dispatch:
    inputs:
      build_commands:
        description: 'Shell commands to execute during the build process (e.g., "npm install && npm run build")'
        required: true
        type: string
        default: 'echo "No custom build commands provided. Skipping build." && exit 0'

jobs:
  build:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest

    steps:
    - name: Checkout repository
      uses: actions/checkout@v4

    - name: Set up Node.js (example)
      uses: actions/setup-node@v4
      with:
        node-version: '18' # Or any other version required by your project

    - name: Execute custom build commands
      run: |
        echo "Executing custom build commands:"
        echo "${{ github.event.inputs.build_commands }}"
        ${{ github.event.inputs.build_commands }}
